trigger.reason: OpenAI unveiled its first custom chip (Jalapeno) built by Broadcom — a major product release from a top-tier lab with high market attention that could trigger sympathy-fade selling in semiconductor supply-chain and AI infrastructure stocks.

primary_event.headline: OpenAI unveils its first custom chip, built by Broadcom
primary_event.summary: OpenAI announced Jalapeno, a custom processor designed specifically for its inference systems and built by Broadcom. This marks OpenAI's entry into proprietary semiconductor design, potentially reducing reliance on third-party chip vendors and signaling vertical integration of AI infrastructure.

secondary_events.headline: Facebook rolls out an AI companion app for creators

discovery.trigger_acknowledgment: Analyzed OpenAI's unveiling of its first custom chip 'Jalapeno' built by Broadcom, a vertical-integration product release that could trigger sympathy-fade across semis and AI infrastructure adjacents.
discovery.run_summary: The trigger is a custom AI inference chip — its true blast radius is GPU vendors and AI chip designers, not software, healthcare IT, or physical security. Several candidates moved sharply negative in directions unrelated to the trigger's actual mechanism (DOCN especially), while others moved UP (VRNS, PHR, EVH, ALRM), making them non-candidates for an OVERDONE-fade BUY signal regardless of threat read.

discoveries.name: DigitalOcean Holdings, Inc.
discoveries.filings_evidence: 10-K explicitly frames AI/ML as a growth vector ('A growing part of our business involves AI/ML products and services') and DOCN operates a developer/SMB-focused IaaS, not a hyperscale frontier-training shop. No filings language ties OpenAI custom-silicon vertical integration to DOCN's revenue streams; their cloud customers are indie devs and SMBs, not OpenAI-scale buyers of inference capacity.
discoveries.thesis: OpenAI building its own inference chip is a vertical-integration story aimed at hyperscale AI inference economics — it has essentially zero direct read-through to DOCN's developer/SMB cloud business. DOCN is being lumped into 'cloud infrastructure providers at risk' by the trigger taxonomy, but its customer base and workload mix don't overlap with what Jalapeno disrupts. The sharp single-day drop on an already-extended week down looks like thematic AI-infra de-risking rather than a justified threat reaction.
discoveries.what_confirms: Stabilization or bounce within 3-5 days once the news cycle moves on; AI/ML revenue disclosures showing continued growth in next 10-Q; no customer concentration disclosure tying DOCN to any frontier lab.
discoveries.what_kills: Disclosure that a meaningful chunk of DOCN's AI/ML compute revenue comes from labs that might in-source (low probability given customer profile); guidance cut citing AI-infra competitive pricing; continued decline suggesting fundamental rather than sympathy selling.
discoveries.what_to_learn: Cloud-infra names get tagged together on every AI-infrastructure headline regardless of customer-segment overlap — SMB/dev-focused clouds are structurally insulated from frontier-lab vertical integration moves but trade like they aren't.
discoveries.catalyst: OpenAI unveils its first custom chip, built by Broadcom
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: DOCN is on AI-infra sympathy lists because it sells IaaS including AI inference, but its SMB/developer customer base bears no resemblance to the hyperscale inference workloads Jalapeno targets.
research_pointers: Check DOCN AI/ML segment revenue disclosures in next 10-Q for actual customer mix
Compare DOCN's 5-day drawdown to NET, FSLY and other developer-cloud peers to confirm sympathy-trade pattern
Verify no concurrent guidance withdrawal or sell-side downgrade today

skipped.reason: Stock moved UP meaningfully on the day — not a sympathy-fade BUY candidate; curation hypothesis (AI beneficiary) appears to be playing out positively.
Stock moved UP on the day — no negative panic to fade.
Stock moved UP on the day — no negative panic to fade.

Direct semi/AI-chip name — within the trigger's actual blast radius (GPU/AI chip designers explicitly named) and curation flagged as edge-AI beneficiary; threat is mixed/real enough that today's modest decline isn't cleanly unjustified given OpenAI-Broadcom signals a path competitors could mimic.
Stock moved UP on the day — no negative panic to fade.
